tool.name: featreport
tool.summary: Report selected features as a stable tabular summary

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: `featreport` summarizes selected features from annotated EMBL or GenBank records into a stable, machine-friendly table. It reuses the shared Epithema feature-selection seam and preserves source-record order followed by feature order.

sections.title: Inputs
sections.content: The current v1 tool accepts one annotated EMBL or GenBank input plus optional `--kind`, `--name`, `--qualifier`, and `--strand` selectors. Selectors combine conjunctively when more than one is supplied, and if none are supplied all features are reported.

sections.title: Outputs
sections.content: The output is a stable table report with one row per selected feature. v1 exposes record identifier, feature kind, normalized location string, 1-based bounds, strand, optional name, and qualifier count.

sections.title: Current Status
sections.content: This method is implemented and exposed through `epithema featreport`. Rust tests currently cover stable ordering, service invocation, and no-match handling through the shared feature-selection and reporting path.

sections.title: Caveats
sections.content: The v1 table is intentionally summary-oriented rather than a full lossless feature-table projection. Multi-span features are reported through a normalized location string, and the report does not attempt to preserve source flatfile formatting.

artifacts.label: Annotated GenBank fixture
artifacts.description: Repository-managed annotated GenBank fixture used for deterministic featreport validation.

examples.title: Report all features from an annotated record
examples.description: Summarizes the two features present in the committed annotated GenBank fixture.

expected_outputs.label: Feature report
expected_outputs.description: One table row is emitted per feature in stable input order.
